commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The branch needs contributor code changes plus contributor-supplied real runtime proof; this is normal PR review rather than a safe ClawSweeper repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff attempts to harden an external SSE response path, but the non-OK SSE bypass leaves a concrete unbounded error-body DoS path.; Review finding: [P1] Bound failed SSE bodies too — src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.ts:38-40 (sha=a47f0b347545b8169aacbd2e13904ffc2ad3e34a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `createSdkBoundedFetch` helper and wires it into the `openai-completions` OpenAI SDK client to cap SSE response bodies at 16 MiB, with helper and provider tests.

PR surface: Source +103, Tests +200. Total +303 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. as a source-reproducible path. Current main constructs the `openai-completions` OpenAI SDK client without a custom fetch, and the inspected OpenAI SDK accumulates SSE chunks until a delimiter and reads failed bodies with `response.text()`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs contributor code changes plus contributor-supplied real runtime proof; this is normal PR review rather than a safe ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: fix(openai-completions): bound SSE response body reads at 16 MiB
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The openai-completions provider creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses from the chat completions API are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory by the SDK internal HTTP layer — an OOM / DoS vector.

This applies the `createSdkBoundedFetch()` wrapper at `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts:606` to cap SSE response body reads at 16 MiB.

## Changes

- `src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.ts` — shared `createSdkBoundedFetch()` function that wraps `fetch` to cap SSE response body reads at 16 MiB (same as #97068).
- `src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.test.ts` — 9 tests covering the bounded fetch wrapper.
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts:606` — inject `fetch: createSdkBoundedFetch()` into the OpenAI SDK constructor.
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` — 2 additional integration tests (33 total in file) verifying the bounded fetch.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: SSE response body reads in the openai-completions provider are now bounded at 16 MiB.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, vitest 4.1.8
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  `pnpm test src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts --run`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  `Test Files 2 passed (2)` / `Tests 42 passed (42)`
- **Observed result after fix**: Oversized SSE response (1536 bytes body, 1024 byte cap) → stream error after 2 chunks, upstream cancelled: true, pulls limited to 2 out of 6.
- **What was not tested**: Did not test live OpenAI API endpoint (would not reproduce a hostile oversized body).

## Out of scope

Companion to #97068 (openai-responses) and #97070 (azure-openai-responses), completing the OpenAI SSE bounded-read set.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection.
- Highest-risk area: false positive on valid large SSE payloads. 16 MiB cap aligns with existing provider bounded-read patterns.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
